comments.text: I think we can go further than just saying a positive answer to the second question implies a positive one to the first. In fact, the two questions are equivalent. For the converse: $\zeta(G)\le \chi(G)$ for every graph $G$, since any proper colouring is a cochromatic colouring using only independent colour classes. Hence, if large chromatic number always forces large dichromatic number, then large cochromatic number also forces large dichromatic number. Indeed, if $\zeta(G)$ is large, then $\chi(G)$ is large, and so $G$ itself has large dichromatic number. Thus $G$ contains a subgraph with large dichromatic number, namely $G$ itself.
